name: dalfox
summary: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.

documentation.readme: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
usage_sections: Usage: Single URL: dalfox scan http://example.com -b https://callback File Mode: dalfox scan urls.txt --custom-payload mypayloads.txt Pipeline: cat urls.txt | dalfox scan --headers "AuthToken: xxx" Check the CLI reference and Quick start documents for more examples.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: brew install dalfox. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `yay -S dalfox`. Further invocations from upstream: `paru -S dalfox`; `nix-shell -p dalfox`. > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security backports. See SECURITY.md for the support policy.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use dalfox because > Looking for the Go (v2.x) version? Dalfox v3 is a complete rewrite in Rust. The Go codebase is preserved on the v2 branch and continues to receive security ba.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `yay -S dalfox` as documented upstream for dalfox.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `paru -S dalfox` as documented upstream for dalfox.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `nix-shell -p dalfox` as documented upstream for dalfox.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `nix run github:hahwul/dalfox -- scan $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for dalfox.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair dalfox with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dalfox --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
